actionable_feedback: Ensure all core calculations are visible in-cell in flow_gating_1.xlsx (no hidden formulas or external links).
Add a single 'Results' tab with direct cell references to each numeric value cited in the memo (e.g., gate spread, min viability, sample_quality_flags, each scenario cost and within-budget flag).
Include a 'Formula manifest' sheet or plain-text export listing the exact formulas used (cell address -> formula) so programmatic tools (openpyxl) can verify reproducibility.
Clarify and document interpretation of the delta_pct values (are negative deltas improvements or stress tests) and whether analyst_count rounding or fractional counts are permitted.
Document the acceptance threshold for analyst_count (if any) and how O3 should be evaluated operationally.
Run and attach a simple programmatic verification (e.g., openpyxl or CSV export) demonstrating the workbook's formulas are readable; include the verification output in the revised submission.
Round or explicitly justify fractional analyst count handling in sensitivity scenarios, or add a note in the memo explaining why fractional analysts were left unrounded.
artifact_specific_comments: expert_memo.md: Complete and well-documented - executive verdict, row-level citations, sensitivity table, assumptions, and review questions are present.
flow_gating_1.xlsx: Present but not programmatically inspectable in the preview environment. Confirm in-cell formula visibility and attach a programmatic verification artifact in the revision.
feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables meet the rubric: the expert memo is thorough, documents calculations and assumptions, and the grader and verifier passed the artifact-contract checks. The only material limitation is that the .xlsx could not be programmatically inspected (openpyxl unavailable), so in-workbook formulas and cell-level visibility were validated via the memo rather than direct cell inspection. Recommend a small, auditability-focused revision to expose formulas, link cells to memo citations, and clarify ambiguous assumptions about analyst_count and the sign/intent of the percent deltas.
